pegar uma cola.reason: The chunk itself is off: Brazilians don't say 'pegar uma cola' to mean 'to cheat with a cheat sheet' — the standard verb is 'colar' (colar na prova = to cheat on the test), with 'cola' as the noun for the cheat sheet used with 'usar'. sent2 'Ela quase pegou uma cola no exame de química' reads as 'she almost obtained/got hold of a cheat sheet', not 'she almost cheated' — a semantic mismatch with the gloss, not a genuine synonym for colar.

passar a mão na cabeça (de).reason: sent1 'O juiz não devia passar a mão na cabeça de criminoso' drops the article/number on 'criminoso' — no Brazilian says it this way in speech; natural phrasing would be 'do criminoso' (singular+article) or 'de criminosos' (plural). As written it reads like clipped headline text, not spoken Portuguese.
